sections.question: Whether or not Council Member Papen should abstain from the selection process for solid waste franchise haulers due to a conflict of interest pursuant to the Political Reform Act, California Government Code Sections 81000, et seq., given that she resides with a gentleman who is a marketing consultant providing services to two competing franchise haulers interested in the solid waste franchise with the City of Diamond Bar.

sections.question_synthetic: Does Council Member Papen have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when the gentleman she resides with (non-spouse) provides marketing services to competing solid waste franchise haulers seeking a contract with the city?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request from City Attorney Andrew V. Arczynski to the FPPC, not an FPPC response/advice letter. The document includes a follow-up supplemental information letter dated August 13, 1990. No FPPC conclusion section is present. OCR quality is moderate with some character recognition issues.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether or not Council Member Papen should abstain from the selection process for solid waste franchise haulers due to a conflict of interest pursuant to the Political Reform Act, California Government Code Sections 81000, et seq., given that she resides with a gentleman who is a marketing consultant providing services to two competing franchise haulers interested in the solid waste franchise with the City of Diamond Bar.

embedding.summary: An incoming request from the City Attorney of Diamond Bar seeking FPPC advice regarding whether Council Member Phyllis Papen should abstain from voting on a solid waste franchise selection due to a potential conflict of interest. The request details that Papen resides with a marketing consultant who has provided services to two competing franchise haulers.
